questions.question: Explain the difference between Warshall's algorithm and Floyd's algorithm with respect to their applications.
keyPoints: Warshall's algorithm computes transitive closure (reachability) - boolean output
Floyd's algorithm computes all-pairs shortest paths - weighted output
Both use similar O(n³) dynamic programming approach
Warshall's uses boolean OR/AND operations; Floyd's uses min operations

questions.question: Write the recurrence relation used in Floyd's algorithm for computing shortest paths.
keyPoints: Where D(k) represents distances using vertices {1,2,...,k} as intermediate vertices
The algorithm considers whether path through vertex k is shorter

questions.question: What is transitive closure? How does Warshall's algorithm compute it?
keyPoints: Transitive closure is the reachability matrix showing which vertices can reach others
Warshall's initializes with direct edges (adjacency matrix)
Iteratively adds paths through intermediate vertices k
Uses R[i][j] = R[i][j] OR (R[i][k] AND R[k][j])

questions.question: What are the initializations done in Floyd's algorithm before the main iterations?
keyPoints: D[i][j] = weight of edge i→j if edge exists
D[i][j] = 0 when i = j (distance to self)
D[i][j] = ∞ (infinity) when no edge exists between i and j
Creates the initial distance matrix from adjacency matrix

questions.question: State the time and space complexities of both Warshall's and Floyd's algorithms.
keyPoints: Time complexity: O(n³) for both algorithms due to three nested loops
Space complexity: O(n²) for both to store n×n matrices
Warshall's: O(n³) time, O(n²) space
Floyd's: O(n³) time, O(n²) space

questions.question: Can Floyd's algorithm handle negative edge weights? Justify your answer.
keyPoints: Yes, Floyd's can handle negative edge weights
It cannot handle negative weight cycles
If negative cycle exists, shortest path becomes undefined (-∞)
Algorithm will give incorrect results for graphs with negative cycles

questions.question: Explain the significance of the variable 'k' in the outer loop of Floyd's algorithm.
keyPoints: k represents the set of intermediate vertices allowed in paths
At iteration k, paths using only vertices {1,2,...,k} as intermediates are considered
k ranges from 1 to n (all vertices)
Progressively builds shortest paths by adding one intermediate vertex at a time

questions.question:
Apply Warshall's algorithm to find the transitive closure of the following adjacency matrix:
[0 1 0; 0 0 1; 1 0 0]. Show all iterations.
keyPoints: Initialize: M = adjacency matrix with direct edges
Iteration k=1: Check paths through vertex 1, update reachable pairs
Iteration k=2: Check paths through vertex 2, update reachable pairs
Iteration k=3: Check paths through vertex 3, update reachable pairs
Final transitive closure matrix shows all reachable vertex pairs
Apply R[i][j] = R[i][j] OR (R[i][k] AND R[k][j])

questions.question:
Apply Floyd's algorithm to find all-pairs shortest paths for the following weighted graph with adjacency matrix:
[0 5 ∞; ∞ 0 3; 2 ∞ 0]. Show the distance matrices for each iteration.
keyPoints: Initialize D(0) with edge weights, 0 on diagonal, ∞ otherwise
Iteration k=1: Consider vertex 1 as intermediate, update D[i][j] = min(D[i][j], D[i][1]+D[1][j])
Iteration k=2: Consider vertex 2 as intermediate, update distances
Iteration k=3: Consider vertex 3 as intermediate, update distances
Final matrix gives shortest distances between all pairs
Show intermediate matrices after each k

questions.question: Compare and contrast Warshall's and Floyd's algorithms under the following headings: (a) Purpose, (b) Type of operations used, (c) Initialization, (d) Output type.
keyPoints: Purpose: Warshall's - reachability/transitive closure; Floyd's - shortest paths
Operations: Warshall's uses boolean OR/AND; Floyd's uses min addition
Initialization: Warshall's uses adjacency matrix; Floyd's uses edge weights/∞/0
Output: Warshall's gives boolean matrix; Floyd's gives distance matrix
Both share O(n³) complexity and dynamic programming approach
Both use adjacency matrix representation

questions.question: Write the pseudo code for both Warshall's and Floyd's algorithms and explain the time complexity analysis.
keyPoints: Warshall's: Initialize R = adjacency matrix; for k=1 to n: for i=1 to n: for j=1 to n: R[i][j] = R[i][j] OR (R[i][k] AND R[k][j])
Floyd's: Initialize D = adjacency matrix; for k=1 to n: for i=1 to n: for j=1 to n: D[i][j] = min(D[i][j], D[i][k]+D[k][j])
Time: Three nested loops each from 1 to n = n³ iterations = O(n³)
Space: Matrices of size n×n = O(n²)
The innermost operation is constant time
